title: Arithmetic Operators
description: Perform basic math: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, modulus.

slides.title: Math in PHP ➕
slides.content:
# Arithmetic Operators

PHP supports all the standard arithmetic operations:

*   `+`  Addition
*   `-`  Subtraction
*   `*`  Multiplication
*   `/`  Division
*   `%`  Modulus (remainder of division)
*   `**` Exponentiation (PHP 5.6+)

They work with integers and floats.

slides.title: Examples
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$a = 10;
$b = 3;

echo $a + $b;
echo $a - $b;
echo $a * $b;
echo $a / $b;
echo $a % $b;
echo $a ** $b;
?>

slides.title: Division by Zero ⚠️
slides.content:
## Beware!

Dividing by zero (or modulo zero) causes a warning and results in `false` for division, and `false` with a warning for modulo. Always check before dividing!

```php
<?php
echo 10 / 0; // Warning: Division by zero
?>
```

quizData.question: What is the result of `17 % 4`?
quizData.explanation: 17 divided by 4 is 4 with remainder 1, so modulus gives 1.
